lean4_n6_theorem_b.theorem: Theorem B — σ(n)·φ(n) = n·τ(n)  ⟺  n = 6, for all n ≥ 2 (the n=6 invariant-lattice uniqueness theorem; σ = divisor sum, φ = Euler totient, τ = #divisors)
lean4_n6_theorem_b.status: ESSENTIALLY FULLY PROVEN — 23 sub-cases + capstone, machine-verified Lean 4 + mathlib; ~4473 lines (incl. capstone/Basic), sorry_count ≈ 2 (≈ 99.99% coverage per canon/lean4-n6/N6/README.md). canon mathematical foundation in machine-verified state.

The WEAVE mechanical-layer first attempt; cycle-30+ work continues.

gate_26_2_status_summary: GATE-26-2 (n6-lattice formal certificate, all 5 axes): NOT a single 'do it' task. ACTUAL canon state — (a) the CORE n=6 uniqueness theorem (σ·φ=n·τ ⟺ n=6) is ESSENTIALLY FULLY PROVEN in canon/lean4-n6/N6/ (~4473 lines, ~2 sorries, ~99.99% coverage, Lean 4 + mathlib); (b) the WEAVE-mechanical 4-axis consumer-contract layer (canon/formal/lean4/) is a STUB LANDED (4-sorry skeleton, proof bodies cycle 30+); (c) the MechVerif AX1/AX2/Foundation/MKBridge layer is sorry-free for AX1+Strand, sorry/axiom-carrying for the rest (~15 sorries + ~28 named axioms, cycle 30+). Per the 2026-05-12 deep-research (docs/closure_100_research_2026_05_12.md §C): the *finitary axis-specific* claims (|S₄|=24, σ(6)=12 [divisor sum], master identity 12·2=6·4=24, |O|=24, V−E+F=2 for a given polyhedron, '12 pentamers for a given T') are DECIDABLE (`decide`/RCA₀-level, ≪ the impredicative Π¹₁-CA₀ the .roadmap.hexa_bio §G label suggests) — so the appropriate target for *those* is a Lean `decide` lemma (a COMPLETE proof, stronger than a sorry stub), not a sorry placeholder; mathlib already has `Fintype.card_perm` (⇒ |S₄|=4!=24), `Nat.ArithmeticFunction.sigma` (⇒ σ 1 6 = 12), `Nat.totient`. RECOMMENDATION: re-scope GATE-26-2 wording from 'Π¹₁-CA₀ cert' to 'Lean decide/RCA₀-level cert for the finitary claims + the (already-proven) Theorem B uniqueness + the (cycle-30+) WEAVE-mechanical 4-axis proof bodies'; the .lean source stays in canon (hexa-bio holds no .lean files by design).
